A fixed-distance trip takes $6$ hours longer at $30$ mph than at $40$ mph. How many hours would the trip take at $60$ mph?
solution_latex: Let the distance be $d$. Then $d/30-d/40=6$, so $d=720$. At $60$ mph the time is $720/60=12$ hours.

prompt_latex: $6$ workers working $5$ hours per day complete a task in $60$ days. If the crew changes to $9$ workers working $4$ hours per day, how many days are needed?
solution_latex: Total worker-hours are $6\cdot 5\cdot 60=1800$. The new time is $1800/(9\cdot 4)=50$ days.
